title: Problem 1.1 (El-Zahar and Erdős)
statement_text: Is the following true? For all integers $t, c \geq 1$, there exists $d \geq 1$, such that if $\chi(G) \geq d$ and $\omega(G) < t$, then there are anticomplete subsets $A, B \subseteq V(G)$ with $\chi(A), \chi(B) \geq c$.
context_text: El-Zahar and Erdős raised this problem in 1986; it remains open. El-Zahar and Erdős themselves proved that under the same hypotheses there are anticomplete subsets $A, B \subseteq V(G)$ with $\chi(A) \geq 3$ and $\chi(B) \geq c$, but little further progress has been made. Omitting the hypothesis on $\omega(G)$ makes the statement false, as a large complete graph shows.
paper_contribution: Proves two partial results: (1.2) for all $t, c \geq 1$ there exists $d$ such that if $\chi(G) \geq d$ and $\omega(G) < t$ then there are anticomplete subsets $A, B$ with $G[A]$ having minimum degree at least $c$ and $\chi(B) \geq c$; and (1.3) a bipartite variant replacing $\omega(G) < t$ by $\tau(G) < t$ (no $K_{t,t}$ subgraph) and the chromatic-number hypothesis by a minimum-degree hypothesis, yielding two anticomplete subgraphs both with minimum degree at least $c$.
notes: PDF source; mathematical notation reads cleanly. The paper title 'On a problem of El-Zahar and Erdős' directly signals this as the central studied conjecture.
paper_title: On a problem of El-Zahar and Erdoos
